content:
That sounds interesting. What I’m building with Continuum operates at a slightly different layer.

Continuum is a local-first publishing node that manages identity (npubs), signed content, relay distribution, and durable archives so authors maintain custody of their writing.

Your approach sounds more like a verification layer anchored to Bitcoin for proving authorship and content lineage. In principle the two ideas could actually complement each other.
